summary:
This patient has developed acute sensorineural hearing loss after receiving neoadjuvant chemotherapy for transitional cell carcinoma of the bladder. Among common agents used in such treatment regimens, platinum-based therapies including cisplatin have been well-documented as causing significant ototoxic side effects due to their ability to generate reactive oxygen species that lead to destruction of inner ear structures via oxidative stress mechanisms.

The correct answer choice reflects an understanding that cisplatin-induced ototoxicity results from direct cellular toxicity mediated by ROS formation rather than other general cytotoxic pathways involving nucleic acid binding or enzyme inhibition alone; thus it best explains both timing and nature of symptoms presented here — consistent with post-chemotherapy onset of progressive bilateral sensorineural deafness observed clinically during therapy cycles.

Final Answer: C
